audit_status.review_note: Reviewed for Jacob at the well, Rachel meeting, Laban recognition, Leah/Rachel exchange, love/service language, hated/unloved Leah, and first four sons' naming wordplays.

verses.restored: Jacob lifted his feet and went to the land of the sons of the east.

familiar.text: Then Jacob went on his journey, and came into the land of the people of the east.

notes.title: Lifted his feet
notes.body: A vivid idiom for setting out after the Bethel encounter.

verses.restored: He looked, and behold, a well in the field, and behold, three flocks of sheep lying beside it, for from that well they watered the flocks. The stone over the mouth of the well was large.

familiar.text: And he looked, and behold a well in the field...

verses.restored: When all the flocks were gathered there, they would roll the stone from the mouth of the well, water the sheep, and return the stone to its place over the mouth of the well.

familiar.text: And thither were all the flocks gathered...

verses.restored: Jacob said to them, My brothers, where are you from? They said, We are from Haran.

familiar.text: And Jacob said unto them, My brethren, whence be ye?...

verses.restored: He said to them, Do you know Laban son of Nahor? They said, We know him.

familiar.text: And he said unto them, Know ye Laban the son of Nahor?...

verses.restored: He said to them, Is there peace with him? They said, Peace. And look, Rachel his daughter is coming with the sheep.

familiar.text: And he said unto them, Is he well? And they said, He is well...

verses.restored: He said, Look, the day is still great; it is not time for the livestock to be gathered. Water the sheep and go pasture them.

familiar.text: And he said, Lo, it is yet high day...

verses.restored: They said, We cannot until all the flocks are gathered and they roll the stone from the mouth of the well; then we water the sheep.

familiar.text: And they said, We cannot, until all the flocks be gathered together...

verses.restored: While he was still speaking with them, Rachel came with her father’s sheep, for she was a shepherdess.

familiar.text: And while he yet spake with them, Rachel came with her father's sheep...

notes.title: Rachel shepherdess
notes.body: Rachel is introduced through active pastoral work.

verses.restored: When Jacob saw Rachel daughter of Laban his mother’s brother, and the sheep of Laban his mother’s brother, Jacob came near, rolled the stone from the mouth of the well, and watered the sheep of Laban his mother’s brother.

familiar.text: And it came to pass, when Jacob saw Rachel...

verses.restored: Jacob kissed Rachel, lifted his voice, and wept.

familiar.text: And Jacob kissed Rachel, and lifted up his voice, and wept.

verses.restored: Jacob told Rachel that he was her father’s brother and that he was Rebekah’s son. She ran and told her father.

familiar.text: And Jacob told Rachel that he was her father's brother...

notes.title: Brother as kin
notes.body: Brother here means close male relative/kinsman.

verses.restored: When Laban heard the report of Jacob his sister’s son, he ran to meet him, embraced him, kissed him, and brought him to his house. Jacob recounted to Laban all these things.

familiar.text: And it came to pass, when Laban heard the tidings of Jacob...

verses.restored: Laban said to him, Surely you are my bone and my flesh. He stayed with him a month of days.

familiar.text: And Laban said to him, Surely thou art my bone and my flesh...

verses.restored: Laban said to Jacob, Because you are my brother, should you serve me for nothing? Tell me, what shall your wages be?

familiar.text: And Laban said unto Jacob, Because thou art my brother...

verses.restored: Now Laban had two daughters. The name of the older was Leah, and the name of the younger was Rachel.

familiar.text: And Laban had two daughters: the name of the elder was Leah...

verses.restored: Leah’s eyes were tender, but Rachel was beautiful in form and beautiful in appearance.

familiar.text: Leah was tender eyed; but Rachel was beautiful and well favoured.

notes.title: Tender eyes
notes.body: Rakot can mean tender, soft, delicate, or weak. The phrase is difficult and should not be flattened.

verses.restored: Jacob loved Rachel. He said, I will serve you seven years for Rachel your younger daughter.

familiar.text: And Jacob loved Rachel; and said, I will serve thee seven years...

verses.restored: Laban said, Better that I give her to you than give her to another man. Stay with me.

familiar.text: And Laban said, It is better that I give her to thee...

verses.restored: Jacob served seven years for Rachel, and they were in his eyes like a few days because of his love for her.

familiar.text: And Jacob served seven years for Rachel; and they seemed unto him but a few days...

verses.restored: Jacob said to Laban, Give me my woman, for my days are completed, and I will go in to her.

familiar.text: And Jacob said unto Laban, Give me my wife, for my days are fulfilled...

verses.restored: Laban gathered all the men of the place and made a feast.

familiar.text: And Laban gathered together all the men of the place, and made a feast.

verses.restored: In the evening he took Leah his daughter and brought her to him, and he went in to her.

familiar.text: And it came to pass in the evening, that he took Leah his daughter...

notes.title: Deceiver deceived
notes.body: Jacob, who deceived his father in darkness of sight, is deceived by Laban at night.

verses.restored: Laban gave Zilpah his slave-woman to Leah his daughter as a slave-woman.

familiar.text: And Laban gave unto his daughter Leah Zilpah his maid...

verses.restored: In the morning, look, it was Leah. He said to Laban, What is this you have done to me? Did I not serve with you for Rachel? Why then have you deceived me?

familiar.text: And it came to pass, that in the morning, behold, it was Leah...

notes.title: What have you done?
notes.body: Jacob’s accusation mirrors the accountability formula used earlier in Genesis.

verses.restored: Laban said, It is not done this way in our place, to give the younger before the firstborn.

familiar.text: And Laban said, It must not be so done in our country...

notes.title: Younger before firstborn
notes.body: Laban’s words sting Jacob’s own story: he, the younger, took the place of the firstborn.

verses.restored: Complete the week of this one, and we will give you that one also for the service that you will serve with me yet another seven years.

familiar.text: Fulfil her week, and we will give thee this also...

verses.restored: Jacob did so and completed her week. Then he gave him Rachel his daughter as his woman.

familiar.text: And Jacob did so, and fulfilled her week...

verses.restored: Laban gave Bilhah his slave-woman to Rachel his daughter as her slave-woman.

familiar.text: And Laban gave to Rachel his daughter Bilhah his handmaid...

verses.restored: He went in also to Rachel, and he loved Rachel more than Leah, and served with him yet another seven years.

familiar.text: And he went in also unto Rachel, and he loved also Rachel more than Leah...

verses.restored: The Eternal saw that Leah was hated/unloved, and he opened her womb, but Rachel was barren.

familiar.text: And when the LORD saw that Leah was hated, he opened her womb...

notes.title: Hated/unloved
notes.body: Senuah can mean hated or unloved in comparison. The text centers Leah’s pain.

verses.restored: Leah conceived and bore a son, and she called his name Reuben, for she said, Because the Eternal has seen my affliction; surely now my man will love me.

familiar.text: And Leah conceived, and bare a son, and she called his name Reuben...

notes.title: Reuben
notes.body: Reuben is linked with see, a son, and the Eternal seeing Leah’s affliction.

verses.restored: She conceived again and bore a son, and said, Because the Eternal has heard that I am hated/unloved, he has given me this one also. She called his name Simeon.

familiar.text: And she conceived again, and bare a son; and said, Because the LORD hath heard...

notes.title: Simeon
notes.body: Simeon is linked with hearing; the Eternal hears Leah’s pain.

verses.restored: She conceived again and bore a son, and said, Now this time my man will be joined to me, because I have borne him three sons. Therefore his name was called Levi.

familiar.text: And she conceived again, and bare a son; and said, Now this time will my husband be joined unto me...

notes.title: Levi
notes.body: Levi is linked with being joined or attached.

verses.restored: She conceived again and bore a son, and said, This time I will praise the Eternal. Therefore she called his name Judah. Then she stopped bearing.

familiar.text: And she conceived again, and bare a son: and she said, Now will I praise the LORD...
